latex:
Janene and Emily plan to go on a marathon training run. Emily arrives late, so Janene starts running 16 minutes before Emily. Janene runs at an average rate of 9 minutes per mile, and Emily runs at an average rate of 8 1/4 minutes per mile. Assuming that both girls started at the same location and ran the same route, how many minutes will Emily take to catch up to Janene?

}

\textbf{Answer:} I solved this algebraicallly:

$$\displaystyle \frac{1}{9} \cdot t = \frac{1}{8.25} \cdot (t-16)$$

$$9 \cdot 8.25 \cdot \displaystyle \frac{1}{9} \cdot t = 9 \cdot 8.25 \cdot  \frac{1}{8.25} \cdot (t-16)$$

$$8.25  \cdot t = 9 \cdot (t-16)$$
$$8.25  \cdot t = 9t - 144$$
$$144  = 0.75\cdot t$$
$$192  =  t$$

Since the question asked for the number of minutes needed to *catch up,* the final step is $192-15=\boxed{176}$ minutes.
